taxonomy: Fusifilum montanum is a species of flowering plant, and its classification falls under the family Asphodelaceae and the genus Fusifilum. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is further classified into the kingdom's subkingdom Phytobionta. More specifically, its classification can be broken down as follows: kingdom Plantae, subkingdom Phytobionta, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Liliopsida, order Asparagales, family Asphodelaceae, and genus Fusifilum. As such, Fusifilum montanum is a monocot plant with a complex classification. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other organisms.

intro: Fusifilum montanum, also known as Chinese goldthread, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Fusifilum, traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Chinese goldthread include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including respiratory infections, digestive issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Chinese goldthread include berberine, palmatine, and epiberberine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chinese goldthread is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as in capsule or tablet form, to harness its health benefits.
